memories.title: REST API v2 Specification
memories.content:
# REST API v2 Specification

## Overview
This document describes the v2 REST API design for our platform.

## Authentication
All endpoints require Bearer token authentication via the `Authorization` header.

```
Authorization: Bearer <token>
```

## Base URL
- Production: `https://api.example.com/v2`
- Staging: `https://api-staging.example.com/v2`

## Endpoints

### Users
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | /users | List all users (paginated) |
| GET | /users/{id} | Get user by ID |
| POST | /users | Create new user |
| PUT | /users/{id} | Update user |
| DELETE | /users/{id} | Delete user |

### Projects
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | /projects | List all projects |
| GET | /projects/{id} | Get project details |
| POST | /projects | Create project |

## Response Format
All responses use JSON with consistent structure:
```json
{
  "data": { ... },
  "meta": {
    "page": 1,
    "pageSize": 20,
    "total": 100
  }
}
```

## Error Handling
Errors return appropriate HTTP status codes with details:
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "VALIDATION_ERROR",
    "message": "Invalid email format",
    "details": { ... }
  }
}
```

memories.title: API Design Review Session - Dec 1, 2025
memories.content:
# API Design Review Session - Dec 1, 2025

## Attendees
- Alice (Tech Lead)
- Bob (Backend Developer)
- Carol (API Architect)

## Agenda
1. Review v2 endpoint design
2. Discuss breaking changes from v1
3. Plan migration strategy

## Discussion Notes

### Authentication Changes
Decided to move from API keys to JWT tokens for better security and session management. Will support API keys for legacy clients for 6 months.

### Pagination
Agreed on cursor-based pagination for large collections. Page-based pagination will be deprecated.

### Versioning Strategy
- URL versioning (v2 in path)
- Sunset header for deprecated endpoints
- 6-month deprecation notice

## Action Items
- [ ] Alice: Update OpenAPI spec with new auth flow
- [ ] Bob: Prototype cursor pagination
- [ ] Carol: Draft migration guide for API consumers

## Next Meeting
December 8, 2025 - Implementation kickoff

memories.title: API Best Practices Reference
memories.content:
# API Best Practices Reference

## RESTful Design Principles

### Resource Naming
- Use nouns, not verbs: `/users` not `/getUsers`
- Use plural forms: `/projects` not `/project`
- Use kebab-case for multi-word: `/user-profiles`

### HTTP Methods
| Method | Usage | Idempotent |
|--------|-------|------------|
| GET | Retrieve resources | Yes |
| POST | Create resources | No |
| PUT | Replace resources | Yes |
| PATCH | Partial update | No |
| DELETE | Remove resources | Yes |

### Status Codes
- 200: Success
- 201: Created
- 204: No Content
- 400: Bad Request
- 401: Unauthorized
- 403: Forbidden
- 404: Not Found
- 422: Unprocessable Entity
- 500: Internal Server Error

## Security Best Practices
1. Always use HTTPS
2. Validate all input
3. Use parameterized queries
4. Implement rate limiting
5. Log security events

memories.content:

## High Priority

## Medium Priority

memories.title: Code Quality Standards
memories.content:
# Code Quality Standards

## Code Review Requirements
1. All PRs require at least one approval
2. No warnings in build output
3. Test coverage must not decrease
4. No TODO comments without linked issues

## Naming Conventions
- Classes: PascalCase
- Methods: PascalCase
- Variables: camelCase
- Constants: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE
- Interfaces: IPrefixed

## Documentation
- Public APIs must have XML docs
- Complex algorithms need inline comments
- README required for each project

## Testing
- Unit tests for business logic
- Integration tests for data access
- Minimum 80% code coverage for new code

memories.title: Cloud Migration Specification
memories.content:
# Cloud Migration Specification

## Current State
- On-premises data center
- VMware virtualization
- Manual deployments
- Single region

## Target State
- AWS cloud infrastructure
- Kubernetes (EKS)
- GitOps with ArgoCD
- Multi-region (us-east-1, us-west-2)

## Migration Phases

### Phase 1: Lift and Shift
Move existing VMs to EC2 with minimal changes.

### Phase 2: Containerization
Package applications as Docker containers.

### Phase 3: Kubernetes
Deploy to EKS with proper orchestration.

### Phase 4: Optimization
Right-size resources, implement auto-scaling.

## Risk Mitigation
- Blue/green deployments
- Gradual traffic shifting
- Automated rollback capability
- 24/7 on-call during migration

memories.title: Q4 Marketing Campaign Plan
memories.content:
# Q4 Marketing Campaign Plan

## Campaign Overview
**Theme**: "End of Year, Start of Savings"
**Duration**: November 15 - December 31, 2025
**Budget**: $50,000

## Target Audience
- Existing customers (upsell)
- Trial users (conversion)
- New prospects (acquisition)

## Channels
| Channel | Budget | Goal |
|---------|--------|------|
| Email | $10,000 | Retention |
| Social | $15,000 | Awareness |
| PPC | $20,000 | Acquisition |
| Content | $5,000 | SEO |

## Key Messages
1. Year-end discount (20% off annual plans)
2. New features announcement
3. Customer success stories

## Success Metrics
- 15% increase in MRR
- 500 new trial signups
- 25% trial-to-paid conversion

memories.title: Brand Style Guide v2.0
memories.content:
# Brand Style Guide v2.0

## Logo Usage
- Minimum size: 32px height
- Clear space: Equal to 'X' height
- Never stretch or distort
- Use provided color variations only

## Color Palette
| Color | Hex | Usage |
|-------|-----|-------|
| Primary Blue | #2563EB | CTAs, headers |
| Secondary Gray | #6B7280 | Body text |
| Accent Green | #10B981 | Success states |
| Warning Orange | #F59E0B | Warnings |
| Error Red | #EF4444 | Errors |

## Typography
- Headings: Inter Bold
- Body: Inter Regular
- Code: JetBrains Mono

## Voice & Tone
- Professional but approachable
- Clear and concise
- Helpful, not condescending
- Technical when needed, simple when possible

memories.title: CRM Integration Requirements (Draft)
memories.content:
# CRM Integration Requirements (Draft)

## Overview
Integrate Salesforce CRM with internal systems for unified customer data.

## Integration Points
1. **Customer Data Sync**
   - Contacts
   - Accounts
   - Opportunities

2. **Activity Tracking**
   - Emails
   - Meetings
   - Support tickets

3. **Reporting**
   - Sales pipeline
   - Customer health scores

## Technical Approach
- Use Salesforce REST API
- Event-driven sync via webhooks
- Daily batch reconciliation

## Open Questions
- [ ] Which Salesforce edition?
- [ ] Data retention requirements?
- [ ] GDPR considerations?
- [ ] Budget for Salesforce licenses?

*This document is a draft and requires stakeholder review.*

memories.title: Incident Response Procedure
memories.content:
# Incident Response Procedure

## Severity Levels

### P1 - Critical
- Service completely down
- Data loss or security breach
- Response: Immediate (15 min)

### P2 - High
- Major feature unavailable
- Performance severely degraded
- Response: 1 hour

### P3 - Medium
- Minor feature issues
- Workaround available
- Response: 4 hours

### P4 - Low
- Cosmetic issues
- Feature requests
- Response: Next business day

## Response Steps

1. **Acknowledge** - Confirm receipt of alert
2. **Assess** - Determine severity and impact
3. **Communicate** - Notify stakeholders
4. **Investigate** - Find root cause
5. **Resolve** - Implement fix
6. **Document** - Write postmortem

## Escalation Path
Engineer → Team Lead → Engineering Manager → CTO

memories.title: Useful CLI Commands
memories.content:
# Useful CLI Commands

## Git
```bash
# Undo last commit but keep changes
git reset --soft HEAD~1

# Interactive rebase last 5 commits
git rebase -i HEAD~5

# Clean up merged branches
git branch --merged | grep -v main | xargs git branch -d
```

## Docker
```bash
# Remove all stopped containers
docker container prune

# Remove unused images
docker image prune -a

# View container logs
docker logs -f container_name
```

## Kubernetes
```bash
# Get all pods in all namespaces
kubectl get pods -A

# Describe a pod
kubectl describe pod pod_name

# Port forward
kubectl port-forward svc/service_name 8080:80
```
